task_completion.description: Log in to OpenCAD as the systems administrator
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenCAD dashboard is visible with the user logged in as admin@opencad.local or an Administrator role.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Data Manager configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data Manager' or 'Admin' settings page is open in the OpenCAD web interface.
The 'Warrant Types' category, tab, or table is selected and displayed within the Data Manager.
The entry 'Civil Contempt' is visible in the list or table of warrant types.
A deletion confirmation dialog for 'Civil Contempt', a success message indicating the warrant type was deleted, or the 'Civil Contempt' entry is visibly absent from the previously viewed list.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenCAD web interface to perform the deletion
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions are performed within the OpenCAD web application, not via direct database clients like phpMyAdmin or MySQL CLI.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the deletion
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element, DOM editor) are not open or used to hide the 'Civil Contempt' row.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open executing SQL queries or scripts to delete the record.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target warrant type to be deleted is 'Civil Contempt'. This is a synthetic entry injected into the system specifically for this task. The deletion must occur through the OpenCAD Data Manager interface.
